reasoning:
The procedure outlined in Section 4 of the 25th Amendment has never been successfully used to remove a sitting US President. It requires that the Vice President and a majority of the Cabinet declare the President unable to discharge the powers and duties of his office; and if the President contests this, a two-thirds vote in both the House and the Senate is required to sustain it. Such a maneuver is extraordinarily difficult and politically fraught.

Currently, as of late October 2025, calls to invoke the 25th Amendment are coming exclusively from political opponents, such as Democratic Governor J.B. Pritzker and Representative Maxine Waters, largely in response to Trump's rhetoric and executive actions. There are no credible reports or signals indicating a rebellion from Vice President JD Vance or the Cabinet, nor are there any confirmed, severe medical emergencies that would genuinely force their hands.

Furthermore, Polymarket odds for Trump's voluntary resignation before the end of 2025 sit at <1%, and the bar for adversarial removal via Section 4 is substantially higher than voluntary resignation. With only two months remaining in 2025 and an intensely loyal Cabinet, the probability of Trump being removed via the 25th Amendment approaches zero. To comply with the boundary requirement of the forecasting system, I provide an estimate of 0.02.
